Building.title: Building
Building.description: Represents a physical building managed by Simpliwork, to which deals are linked.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Building entity.

name.description: The official name of the building.

city.description: The city where the building is located.

cluster.description: The geographical or logical cluster the building belongs to.

activeStatus.description: Indicates if the building is currently active and available for new deals.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the Simpliwork Deal OS, with specific roles and permissions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and identification.

role.description: The assigned role of the user (Sales, Design, Management, Admin).

activeStatus.description: Indicates if the user account is currently active.

Deal.title: Deal
Deal.description: Represents a qualified sales opportunity being tracked by Simpliwork Deal OS.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Deal entity.

companyName.description: The name of the client company associated with this deal.

buildingId.description: Reference to the Building entity this deal is linked to. (Relationship: Building 1:N Deal)

salesOwnerEmail.description: The email address of the Sales user who owns and manages this deal. (Reference to User's email).

stage.description: The current stage of the deal in the sales pipeline (e.g., Qualified, Solutioning, Proposal Sent, Negotiation, LoI Initiated, LoI Signed, Lost).

requirementSummary.description: A detailed summary of the client's requirements for the deal.

approxRequirementSize.description: The approximate size or quantity of the client's requirement (e.g., square footage, number of units).

sourceType.description: The category of the source from which the deal originated (e.g., Direct, Referral, Marketing).

sourceName.description: The specific name or identifier of the deal's source.

createdDate.description: The date and time when the deal record was first created.

lastActivityDate.description: The date and time of the last recorded activity or update on the deal.

budgetClarity.description: Indicates whether the client's budget for the deal is clear and understood by Sales.

timelineClarity.description: Indicates whether the client's timeline for project execution is clear and understood by Sales.

decisionMakerIdentified.description: Indicates whether the key decision-maker(s) on the client side have been identified.

layoutRequestedDate.description: The date when a layout design or solutioning support was formally requested from Design.

layoutUploadedDate.description: The date and time when the final (or latest) layout design file was uploaded by the Design team.

layoutRevisionCount.description: The total number of revisions made to the layout design for this deal.

designNote.description: Notes and comments from the Design team regarding the solutioning or layout specifics.

layoutFileUpload.description: A URL or path to the uploaded layout design file, if applicable.

loiInitiatedDate.description: The date when the Letter of Intent (LoI) process was formally initiated with the client.

loiSignedDate.description: The date when the Letter of Intent (LoI) was officially signed by both parties.

lostReason.description: The reason why the deal was marked as 'Lost', if applicable.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Each document is identified by the user's Firebase Auth UID. The 'role' field is used for UI logic and backend processing, while explicit role collections are used for security rule authorization. Access is typically restricted to the user themselves and Admin users.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) for the user.

definition.description: Master data for Simpliwork buildings. Documents are created and managed by Admin users. All other authenticated roles have read-only access. The 'id' field within the schema corresponds to 'buildingId' in the path.

params.name: buildingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the building.

definition.description: Main collection for tracking qualified deals. Each deal document includes denormalized 'salesOwnerId' (Firebase UID of the sales owner) for authorization independence, and 'stage' for QAP-compliant filtering for Design users. Access is strictly controlled by role and ownership.

params.name: dealId
params.description: The unique identifier for the deal.

definition.description: Authorization collection: The existence of a document in this collection signifies that the associated 'userId' (Firebase UID) has the 'Admin' role, granting full read/write privileges across the application. Used for Authorization Independence via 'Existence over Content'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of an Admin user.

definition.description: Authorization collection: The existence of a document in this collection signifies that the associated 'userId' (Firebase UID) has the 'Management' role, granting read-only 'God View' access to all deals and buildings. Used for Authorization Independence via 'Existence over Content'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of a Management user.

definition.description: Authorization collection: The existence of a document in this collection signifies that the associated 'userId' (Firebase UID) has the 'Sales' role, granting permissions to create and manage their own deals. Used for Authorization Independence via 'Existence over Content'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of a Sales user.

definition.description: Authorization collection: The existence of a document in this collection signifies that the associated 'userId' (Firebase UID) has the 'Design' role, granting specific read access to 'Solutioning' stage deals and limited update capabilities. Used for Authorization Independence via 'Existence over Content'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of a Design user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be highly secure, scalable, and debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
1.  **Deal Ownership:** For the `/deals/{dealId}` collection, the `salesOwnerId` (Firebase Authentication UID of the sales owner) is denormalized directly into each `Deal` document. This is critical for authorization independence, as it allows security rules to evaluate ownership (e.g., `resource.data.salesOwnerId == request.auth.uid`) without requiring `get()` calls to the `/users` collection or other parent documents. This enables atomic operations and simplifies rule logic, aligning with the CRITICAL mandate to avoid hierarchical authorization dependencies.
2.  **Role-Based Access (DBAC):** Instead of relying on a 'role' field within the `/users/{userId}` document for primary authorization, dedicated role collections are used: `/roles_admin/{userId}`, `/roles_management/{userId}`, `/roles_sales/{userId}`, and `/roles_design/{userId}`. The mere existence of a document for a user's `uid` in these collections grants the respective role. This 'Existence over Content' approach satisfies DBAC (roles stored in DB, relying on `request.auth.uid`) and Authorization Independence (rules use `exists()` checks, not `get()` on user profiles for roles).

**Support for Query Against Permitted Subsets (QAPs):**
1.  **Management (God View):** Management users are granted read access to *all* documents in `/deals/{dealId}` via an `exists(/roles_management/$(request.auth.uid))` check. This fully supports the
